old_claim.note: Pharmacopoeia Compilation provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: Mechanical tablet compression retains machine-tool and mass-production dependencies; Pliny's encyclopedia is unrelated to the mechanism.

source_shape.source_locator: Britannica describes industrial drug development and dosage-form manufacture but does not tie tablet compression to Pliny's Natural History.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The industrial pharmacy overview supports manufacturing context while leaving the claimed ancient-compendium dependency unsupported.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The corrected scope leaves this relationship outside the causal boundary. Mechanical tablet compression retains machine-tool and mass-production dependencies; Pliny's encyclopedia is unrelated to the mechanism.

ontology_before: tablet_pressing treated pharmacopoeia_compilation as a direct enabling relationship.
ontology_after: Mechanical tablet compression retains machine-tool and mass-production dependencies; Pliny's encyclopedia is unrelated to the mechanism.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: tablet_pressing no longer has pharmacopoeia_compilation as a direct prerequisite.
would_reject_if: A direct historical or technical source establishes pharmacopoeia_compilation as a necessary causal input to the newly scoped tablet_pressing claim.
short_reason: Mechanical tablet compression retains machine-tool and mass-production dependencies; Pliny's encyclopedia is unrelated to the mechanism.
